name: TDD-SUBAGENT-BACKLOG-17
title: Wave 7 historical-burden sweep: drop legacy-flag tombstones + reframe HANDOFF YAML
description:
Wave 7 deletion-biased sweep. Drop 'legacy/removed' tombstone prose from active spec/contract, and reframe the YAML HANDOFF block as 'human-readable view' (not 'backward-compatible parsers').

SPEC.md TRIMS:
- L74-77: drop 'legacy bug-status.py/backlog-item-status.py consolidated' clause (active path already named on L71)
- L107-109: drop 'legacy --no-human-approval flag is removed; callers must use --human-approval-gate' (argparse rejects unknown flags naturally)
- L233-236: drop 'except where backward-compat test exercises it' escape hatch (no live consumer); state simply 'fixtures use flat shape'
- L243-244: reframe 'legacy YAML-like HANDOFF block (for human readability and backward-compatible dispatchers)' to active 'YAML HANDOFF block is the human-readable view; JSON block is the machine-first source of truth'

contract.md:
- L22: drop 'Legacy bug-dispatch and backlog-dispatch positional flags have been removed' tombstone clause

scripts/dispatch-tdd-subagent.py:
- L124 help text: drop 'Replaces the legacy --no-human-approval flag' parenthetical
- L282 comment: change 'legacy YAML block above' → 'human-readable YAML block above'
- L582-586 emit comment: replace 'for human readability and backward-compatible parsers' with 'human-readable view alongside the machine-first JSON'

test/test-dispatch-linked-items.py:
- t6 (L170-182): rename 'backward compat — singular --linked-item' framing to 'primary --linked-item alone produces single close path'. Test logic stays.

PRESERVED: --linked-item is the live primary B/B-cycle interface (used by every backlog dispatch in rabbit-feature). HANDOFF YAML block itself stays — rabbit-feature-touch Step 6 reads it visually.
